core_mechanism: the effect of quenched (frozen-in) structural disorder on the order of a phase transition — whether frozen randomness in the medium leaves a discontinuous (first-order) transition intact or rounds it into a continuous (second-order) one, with the outcome depending on the type of disorder
brief_reason: The abstract contrasts how quenched coordination-number (geometric) randomness versus quenched bond randomness act on a system whose transition is discontinuous on regular lattices, finding via extensive stochastic (Monte Carlo) ensemble sampling that the geometric disorder preserves the first-order transition while bond disorder softens it to second order — i.e. disorder-controlled change in the order of a phase transition.
